required_fields.label: Fixed expenses (one per line)
required_fields.help: category | amount — housing, utilities, insurance, subscriptions

required_fields.label: Variable expenses (one per line)
required_fields.help: category | amount — groceries, transport, dining, discretionary

file_upload_prompts.label: Bank / brokerage statements, transaction CSVs (optional — multiple allowed)

sections.label: Header
sections.instructions:
Voice: Personal-financial neutral. Factual. Use the person's name in third person ("[Name]'s income for [month] totaled..." / "[Name] saved..."). Never editorialize on choices ("[Name] should reduce dining out"). Do not assess goodness or badness; report the numbers. The summary is a record of what was earned, spent, saved, and owed during the month — neutrally presented for the person's own review and planning.

PIPELINE OWNS THE HEADER (person_name, month_label, prepared_date in masthead). Do NOT emit a title or section that duplicates the masthead. Start your output directly with the Income section heading.

=== CRITICAL OUTPUT RULES ===

RULE 1: DO NOT emit the document title as <h1> or in any form. The pipeline adds the title on the cover or masthead. Any title in your body HTML creates a duplicate.

RULE 2: DO NOT emit decorative banners, spaced-out letter wordmarks, document IDs, brand names above the first section, or any content before the first <h2> heading. Start directly with the first section heading.

RULE 3: Every section MUST have an <h2> heading with the exact title from the 'sections' array. No section may appear without a heading. Do not let content drift into unlabeled prose.

RULE 4: Emit sections in the exact order listed. Do not skip required sections. Conditional sections (those gated on user input flags) may be omitted only when the input does not include them.

=== LENGTH ===
400–800 words of prose. Tables provide most of the substance; commentary is brief and neutral.

HEADER: write this section per the document type's standard structure.

sections.label: Income
sections.instructions:
INCOME: <table class="fin-table"> with columns: Source | Amount. Parse income_sources input as pipe-separated "source | amount". One row per source. Subtotal row using class "subtotal-row" labeled "Total Income" with the sum.
Format all currency consistently: $X,XXX.XX with thousand separators and two decimals.

sections.label: Expenses
sections.instructions:
EXPENSES: Two distinct sub-sections rendered as

:

Fixed Expenses

— with columns Category | Amount. Parse fixed_expenses input. Rows for housing, utilities, insurance, subscriptions, etc. Subtotal "Subtotal — Fixed Expenses".

Variable Expenses

—
with columns Category | Amount. Parse variable_expenses input. Rows for groceries, transport, dining, discretionary, etc. Subtotal "Subtotal — Variable Expenses". After both sub-tables, a single combined total row: "Total Expenses: $[sum of both subtotals]" — rendered as a paragraph or as a closing line in a small summary table.

sections.label: Savings and Debt
sections.instructions:
SAVINGS AND DEBT: Two sub-sections rendered as

:

Savings Deposits

— ONLY emit if savings_deposits input is non-empty. with columns Account | Amount. Subtotal "Total Savings Deposits".

Debt Payments

— ONLY emit if debt_payments input is non-empty.
with columns Debt | Amount | Type (Principal / Interest). Subtotal "Total Debt Payments". If both sub-sections are empty (no savings, no debt activity), emit a brief paragraph: "No savings deposits or debt payments recorded for [month_label]."

sections.label: Net Cash Flow
sections.instructions:
NET CASH FLOW: Single summary block as a small <table class="fin-table">:
  Total Income: $X
  Less: Total Expenses: -$Y
  Less: Savings Deposits: -$Z (if savings section was emitted)
  Less: Debt Payments: -$W (if debt section was emitted)
  ─────
  Net Cash Flow: $(X - Y - Z - W)
The "Net Cash Flow" row uses class "total-row" for emphasis. State the result one of three ways:
  - Positive: "[Name]'s net cash flow for [month_label] is positive at $[amount], available for additional savings, debt reduction, or carry-forward."
  - Zero: "[Name]'s income matched outflows exactly for [month_label]."
  - Negative: "[Name]'s net cash flow for [month_label] is negative at $[amount]; outflows exceeded income."
Keep the framing factual.

sections.label: Net Worth Snapshot
sections.instructions:
NET WORTH SNAPSHOT: ONLY emit if assets_snapshot or liabilities_snapshot input is non-empty. Render as two parallel sub-tables (or columns):

Assets

— list with values, total at the bottom.

Liabilities

— list with balances, total at the bottom. Then a final line: "Net Worth ([prepared_date]): Assets $[A] − Liabilities $[L] = $[A − L]." If only assets provided (no liabilities) or vice versa, state "Liabilities not reported in this summary" or similar. The net worth figure uses class "total-row" treatment.

sections.label: Goals and Notable Events
sections.instructions:
GOALS AND NOTABLE EVENTS: ONLY emit if goals_progress or notable_events input is non-empty.

Goals Progress

— only if goals_progress provided. with columns Goal | Target | Actual | Status. Status values: On Track / Ahead / Behind / Achieved.

Notable Events

— only if notable_events provided. Bulleted list of one-time events that affected this month's finances (bonus, refund, large purchase, medical event).

sections.label: Next Month Outlook
sections.instructions: NEXT MONTH OUTLOOK: ONLY emit if next_month_adjustments input is non-empty. Brief paragraph (2–4 sentences) describing planned adjustments for the upcoming month: changes to budget categories, planned large expenses or income, new savings or debt-payment targets. No prescriptive language.
